name: Parcha Bozbash- Lamb Soup w/ Chestnuts, Quince,& Prunes
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Boneless lean lamb (cut into 1-inch cubes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the lamb with salt and pepper.
2. In a heavy pot, melt 3 tbsp butter over moderate heat.
3. Add the lamb and onion; sauté until browned, stirring frequently.
4. Add the beef broth. Adjust seasoning as needed, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes.
5. Add the potato, quince, prunes, and chestnuts. Cover and simmer for another 30 minutes.
6. Add the chick-peas and simmer, covered, for an additional 20 minutes.
7. Stir in the clarified butter until melted before serving the soup.
Note: You may add a pinch of powdered saffron or ginger dissolved in warm water to the chick-peas, if desired. The soup can be served sprinkled with crushed dried mint, accompanied by chopped onion and sumakh on the side. In the Caucasus, ground kyurdyuk is often used instead of clarified butter, and dried chick-peas are preferred over canned ones. Soak the dried chick-peas for 4-6 hours before using, then drain them before adding to the broth at the beginning of cooking.
